name: Flooded Street Corridor — Flood Street to Bridge Approach
description: Two blocks of residential street between the Flood Street house and the St. Claude Bridge approach, currently under approximately three feet of water; navigable by kayak in a straight line but the submerged street furniture — stop signs, parked cars, porch steps — requires active reading of the water surface.
atmosphericDetails: The water surface is nearly flat here — the current from the original breach has long since dissipated, leaving a still, opaque grey plane that conceals everything below knee depth
The tops of stop signs and utility poles provide navigation reference points; the street grid is recoverable if you grew up here
The EPA classified this water as hazardous — fecal coliform, E. coli, heavy metals, petroleum hydrocarbons; any open wound in contact with this water for more than a few minutes is a clinical event
The smell at water level is the full compound — organic, chemical, petroleum, biological — without the slight relief of elevation that the bridge deck provides
